title: Scan for secrets before every commit
description: Run a secret scanner in a pre-commit hook and in CI so a credential is caught before it ever reaches shared history.
body: Use when setting up a repo or hardening an existing one. Do not rely on reviewers to spot secrets by eye. Actions: install a scanner such as gitleaks as a pre-commit hook; run the same scan in CI as a required gate; tune rules to cut false positives without disabling detection. Validation signals include a commit introducing a high-entropy key being blocked locally and the CI gate failing on a planted secret. Risk boundary: never weaken or bypass the scanner to land a commit faster, and if a hit is a real secret, treat it as a leak and rotate rather than allowlisting it.
